metadata.summary: pytest-asyncio enables async Python testing. Covers asyncio_mode auto vs strict, async fixtures via pytest_asyncio.fixture, AsyncMock, timeout handling, FastAPI client, and common async gotchas.

content.title: pytest Async Testing — pytest-asyncio, Async Fixtures, and FastAPI
content.summary: pytest-asyncio adds an event loop and @pytest.mark.asyncio support so async def test functions run as coroutines. In auto mode every async test and fixture is treated as asyncio automatically. Use pytest_asyncio.fixture for async fixtures and AsyncMock for async callables.

why.p: Async def test functions are coroutines — pytest cannot run them directly without an event loop. pytest-asyncio wraps each async test in asyncio.run() (or a shared loop for wider scopes) and integrates with the fixture system so async fixtures can yield and clean up like sync fixtures. Without this plugin, await inside a test body raises a SyntaxError or the test returns a coroutine object that is truthy and the test silently passes without executing.

list.item: Testing FastAPI or any ASGI application with httpx.AsyncClient and ASGITransport.
Testing async functions that call async libraries: httpx, asyncpg, aioredis, motor.
Testing asyncio.gather, asyncio.TaskGroup, or ExceptionGroup handling.
Testing timeout and cancellation behavior with asyncio.wait_for or asyncio.timeout.
Any test that contains await expressions.
Pure sync code — installing pytest-asyncio adds no value and asyncio_mode="auto" silently wraps sync tests in no-op coroutine handling.
Mixing sync Django ORM calls directly in async tests — the ORM blocks the event loop and looks like a hang; use sync_to_async or a separate sync test.
Tests that only call sync adapters of an otherwise async service — call the sync adapter synchronously.

section.name: asyncio-mode
section.p: pytest-asyncio has two modes configurable via asyncio_mode in pyproject.toml:

list.item: auto: all async def test_ functions and fixtures are treated as asyncio coroutines automatically. No @pytest.mark.asyncio needed.
strict (default before 0.21): only functions explicitly marked with @pytest.mark.asyncio are treated as async tests.

rule.statement: Set asyncio_mode = "auto" in pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] for projects where most tests are async. Do not mix asyncio_mode="auto" with explicit @pytest.mark.asyncio markers — the redundant markers are harmless but add noise. For projects with a small minority of async tests, use strict mode and mark only those tests.
rule.rationale: Flipping asyncio_mode breaks marker expectations silently — strict mode ignores unmarked async tests (the test body never runs, the test passes). auto mode is the right default for FastAPI projects; strict is appropriate for Django projects where most tests are sync.

section.name: async-fixtures
section.p: Async fixtures use @pytest_asyncio.fixture (not @pytest.fixture). They can yield like sync fixtures, and teardown (after yield) runs in the same event loop as setup.

example.title: Async fixtures for FastAPI client and database
Basic async test and concurrent gather
AsyncMock for async external service and exception handling
example.note: Use assert_awaited_once() not assert_called_once() — the latter does not verify the coroutine was awaited.

example.title: Testing timeout and cancellation behavior

section.name: fastapi-conftest-template
section.p: A minimal conftest.py pattern for FastAPI with SQLAlchemy async:

example.title: FastAPI pytest conftest.py with dependency override

checklist.check: Install pytest-asyncio and set asyncio_mode = "auto" in pyproject.toml for async-heavy projects.
Use AsyncMock (not Mock) for any async callable being patched.
Use assert_awaited_once() to verify an async mock was both called and awaited.
Test timeout behavior explicitly with asyncio.wait_for or asyncio.timeout.
Avoid mixing sync Django ORM calls in async tests — wrap with sync_to_async.
Check event loop lifecycle — session-scoped async fixtures share one loop; function-scoped use a fresh one.
Run pytest --collect-only to surface conftest.py import errors early.

section.name: ai-agent-gotchas
section.p: Async testing mistakes agents commonly make:

list.item: Forgotten await in async tests — assertion runs against a coroutine object, evaluates as truthy, passes silently. Ruff ASYNC rules catch some of these.
Using @pytest.fixture instead of @pytest_asyncio.fixture for async fixtures — the fixture body never runs as a coroutine.
Using Mock() instead of AsyncMock() for async callables — the await expression succeeds but asserts nothing.
Mixing asyncio_mode="auto" with explicit @pytest.mark.asyncio — redundant but harmless; avoid adding noise.
pytest-asyncio mode (auto vs strict) config drift — flipping breaks marker expectations silently.
pytest-django db fixture with async tests — the sync Django ORM blocks the event loop and looks like a timeout; use sync_to_async or pytest-django's async_to_sync_db.
